title: `podman quadlet install <file/url>` should support tarball
body:
### Feature request description

I would like to be able to able to directly pass a url pointing to a tarball from the `podman quadlet install` command. 

I'm not sure if this is not yet implement as the documentation of the command says the following

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/b12c51e5a43ae3a8c9ef8446ba5eb8fc9060471b/cmd/podman/quadlet/install.go#L15

The last part in _"Quadlets may be specified as local files, Web URLs, and OCI artifacts"_  would suggest I can provide an OCI artifact, but there is no examples, and I don't seems to see the implementation in the code? So might be something that is not yet here?

### Suggest potential solution

We should be able to specify an OCI image or a an url pointing to a tar.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

The alternatives are to download, untar, and install the directory

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body:
I've been looking at the quadlet install logic recently and I'd love to implement this. 

I'll start looking into wiring up tarball extraction and URL  fetching for this command. 

Could you assign it to me?

Hey @Honny1 I wanted to have some clarification on the expected implementation details

- Supported format

The name of the issue (I created) mention `tarball` but as the documentation currently mention `OCI artifact`[^1] which direction should we go in? Should we support .tar.gz or OCI artifact?

- Archive structure

Currently with the `podman quadlet install` command we can specify a directory as first argument, but only the files at the root of this directory will be copied, the install is not recursive[^2]. 

This problem translate to the archive, if we consider passing an archive url as first argument, we download the archive, extract it and run the same logic as a directory, but currently we would only copy the files at the root.

Do we want to support copying the full _directory / OCI / archive / tarball_ with all its content including sub directories?

For example copying an archive with the following content

```
/ nginx.tar
└── nginx/
    ├── nginx.container
    └── nginx.config
```

should result in the following

```
/home/user/.config/containers/systemd
└── nginx/
    ├── nginx.container
    └── nginx.config
```

> ℹ️ the quadlet systemd generator read recursively the directory, meaning this would be valid without any change required on the quadlet binary side.

[^1]: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/b12c51e5a43ae3a8c9ef8446ba5eb8fc9060471b/cmd/podman/quadlet/install.go#L15
[^2]: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/4c3027c149ad54dbb6f96328694e7b852623b4b8/pkg/domain/infra/abi/quadlet.go#L147-L162

> * Supported format
> 
> The name of the issue (I created) mention `tarball` but as the documentation currently mention `OCI artifact`[1](#user-content-fn-1-e50ca27c157e47a8c4fd95fd05201001) which direction should we go in? Should we support .tar.gz or OCI artifact?

I think this issue should focus on tarball support. It seems the documentation wasn't fully cleaned up; there was an intention to support `OCI artifacts`, but it appears to have been overlooked. I believe `OCI artifact` support should be handled in a separate issue and PR. https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26330#discussion_r2152013820

> * Archive structure
> 
> Currently with the `podman quadlet install` command we can specify a directory as first argument, but only the files at the root of this directory will be copied, the install is not recursive[2](#user-content-fn-2-e50ca27c157e47a8c4fd95fd05201001).
> 
> This problem translate to the archive, if we consider passing an archive url as first argument, we download the archive, extract it and run the same logic as a directory, but currently we would only copy the files at the root.
> 
> Do we want to support copying the full _directory / OCI / archive / tarball_ with all its content including sub directories?

I think we should support full _directories, OCI images?, archives, and tarballs_ including all subdirectories. I don't see any specific reason not to. However, that might be better suited for a separate PR and issue.


cc @flouthoc @mheon @ygalblum @Luap99

I think recursive-copy makes sense, if we're going to install a directory we should install all of it.
However, I think we have a reasonable expectation that only Quadlets in the top-level directory will be installed.

I think supporting OCI archive makes sense but it requires a specific design, IMO it should use their own media/artifact type to define our own standard and not just any artifact with random files. But agree that discussion should happen in a new issue.

As for tarball support I think we have the usual we must make sure to extract correctly within the root, no symlinks escapes must be allowed. Is there a use cases for nested directories within the tarball, just to set the dir name seems like there could be other ways?

Personally I do not like the current quadlet install behavior of dumping all into the quadlet root dir and inventing these bespoke .app and .asset files? I guess it would be much more logic if quadlet install would by default dump everything into a subdirectory and treat each dir as app without needing extra tracking files. We could by default infer the directory name from the input and otherwise have a cli option to chnage the name?
